nodes.x: My son, attend unto my wisdom, and bow thine ear to my understanding:
So that thou incline thine ear unto wisdom, and apply thine heart to understanding;
¶ My son, attend to my words; incline thine ear unto my sayings.
And again, verily I say unto you, to some is given, by the Spirit of God, the word of wisdom.
My son, give ear to my words; for I swear unto you, that inasmuch as ye shall keep the commandments of God ye shall prosper in the land.
O, remember, my son, and learn wisdom in thy youth; yea, learn in thy youth to keep the commandments of God.
That thou mayest regard discretion, and that thy lips may keep knowledge.
Discretion shall preserve thee, understanding shall keep thee:
That thou mightest know the certainty of those things, wherein thou hast been instructed.
Having confidence in thy obedience I wrote unto thee, knowing that thou wilt also do more than I say.
That I might make thee know the certainty of the words of truth; that thou mightest answer the words of truth to them that send unto thee?
My lips shall utter praise, when thou hast taught me thy statutes.
¶ For the lips of a strange woman drop as an honeycomb, and her mouth is smoother than oil:
Lying lips are abomination to the Lord: but they that deal truly are his delight.
The mouth of strange women is a deep pit: he that is abhorred of the Lord shall fall therein.
Thy lips, O my spouse, drop as the honeycomb: honey and milk are under thy tongue; and the smell of thy garments is like the smell of Lebanon.

"At school we had been great friends; she was a year older than myself,
and as great a contrast in appearance as in disposition.  Her short,
black, and rather coarse hair curled round a pretty forehead; she had a
dark, clear complexion--the colour of cream in which a few drops of
coffee have been mixed; a bright, deep colour; full, pretty-shaped,
pouting lips, ever ready and ever seeming ready…
A lying tongue hateth those that are afflicted by it; and a flattering mouth worketh ruin.
But her end is bitter as wormwood, sharp as a twoedged sword.
He hath filled me with bitterness, he hath made me drunken with wormwood.
And I find more bitter than death the woman, whose heart is snares and nets, and her hands as bands: whoso pleaseth God shall escape from her; but the sinner shall be taken by her.
Ye who turn judgment to wormwood, and leave off righteousness in the earth,
And the anger of the Lord is kindled, and his sword is bathed in heaven, and it shall fall upon the inhabitants of the earth.
She maketh fine linen, and selleth it; and delivereth girdles unto the merchant.
Her feet go down to death; her steps take hold on hell.
Her house is the way to hell, going down to the chambers of death.
For her house inclineth unto death, and her paths unto the dead.
God is in the midst of her; she shall not be moved: God shall help her, and that right early.
And if thy foot offend thee, cut it off: it is better for thee to enter halt into life, than having two feet to be cast into hell, into the fire that never shall be quenched:
Which holdeth our soul in life, and suffereth not our feet to be moved.
Lest thou shouldest ponder the path of life, her ways are moveable, that thou canst not know them.
That thou shouldest take it to the bound thereof, and that thou shouldest know the paths to the house thereof?
¶ Therefore, behold, I will hedge up thy way with thorns, and make a wall, that she shall not find her paths.
I know that thou canst do every thing, and that no thought can be withholden from thee.
Ponder the path of thy feet, and let all thy ways be established.
¶ Go to the ant, thou sluggard; consider her ways, and be wise:
Hear me now therefore, O ye children, and depart not from the words of my mouth.
¶ Hearken unto me now therefore, O ye children, and attend to the words of my mouth.
Now therefore hearken unto me, O ye children: for blessed are they that keep my ways.
Yet hear the word of the Lord, O ye women, and let your ear receive the word of his mouth, and teach your daughters wailing, and every one her neighbour lamentation.
If I say, I will speak thus; behold, I should offend against the generation of thy children.
Hear, O heavens, and give ear, O earth: for the Lord hath spoken, I have nourished and brought up children, and they have rebelled against me.
Remove thy way far from her, and come not nigh the door of her house:
Come out of her, my people, that ye be not partakers of her sine, and that ye receive not of her
Take fast hold of instruction; let her not go: keep her; for she is thy life.
Lest thou give thine honour unto others, and thy years unto the cruel:
For I will promote thee unto very great honour, and I will do whatsoever thou sayest unto me: come therefore, I pray thee, curse me this people.

And it shall come to pass, because of the wickedness of the world, that I will take vengeance upon the wicked, for they will not repent; for the cup of mine indignation is full; for behold, my blood shall not cleanse them if they hear me not.
Both riches and honour come of thee, and thou reignest over all; and in thine hand is power and might; and in thine hand it is to make great, and to give strength unto all.
Not unto us, O Lord, not unto us, but unto thy name give glory, for thy mercy, and for thy truth’s sake.
Also unto thee, O Lord, belongeth mercy: for thou renderest to every man according to his work.
Lest strangers be filled with thy wealth; and thy labours be in the house of a stranger;
¶ Thou shalt not oppress an hired servant that is poor and needy, whether he be of thy brethren, or of thy strangers that are in thy land within thy gates:
Let them be only thine own, and not strangers’ with thee.
And thou mourn at the last, when thy flesh and thy body are consumed,
And it shall come to pass in that day that the Lord shall give thee rest, from thy sorrow, and from thy fear, and from the hard bondage wherein thou wast made to serve.
And it shall come to pass in the day that the Lord shall give thee rest from thy sorrow, and from thy fear, and from the hard bondage wherein thou wast made to serve,
But his flesh upon him shall have pain, and his soul within him shall mourn.
And the fruits that thy soul lusted after are departed from thee, and all things which were dainty and goodly are departed from thee, and thou shalt find them no more at all.
And say, How have I hated instruction, and my heart despised reproof;
Whoso loveth instruction loveth knowledge: but he that hateth reproof is brutish.
Seeing thou hatest instruction, and castest my words behind thee.
A fool despiseth his father’s instruction: but he that regardeth reproof is prudent.
To give instruction, where instruction’s voice Will guide the feel and make the heart rejoice—
He that refuseth instruction despiseth his own soul: but he that heareth reproof getteth understanding.
And have not obeyed the voice of my teachers, nor inclined mine ear to them that instructed me!
And they have turned unto me the back, and not the face: though I taught them, rising up early and teaching them, yet they have not hearkened to receive instruction.
But they obeyed not, neither inclined their ear, but made their neck stiff, that they might not hear, nor receive instruction.
I have declared my ways, and thou heardest me: teach me thy statutes.
Unto me men gave ear, and waited, and kept silence at my counsel.
Thy first father hath sinned, and thy teachers have transgressed against me.
I was almost in all evil in the midst of the congregation and assembly.
I have hated the congregation of evil doers; and will not sit with the wicked.

And because they did cast them all out, that there were none righteous among them, I did send down fire and destroy them, that their wickedness and abominations might be hid from before my face, that the blood of the prophets and the saints whom I sent among them might not cry unto me from the ground against them.
Or else let these same here say, if they have found any evil doing in me, while I stood before the council,
I the Lord have said, I will surely do it unto all this evil congregation, that are gathered together against me: in this wilderness they shall be consumed, and there they shall die.
Then shall ye remember your own evil ways, and your doings that were not good, and shall lothe yourselves in your own sight for your iniquities and for your abominations.
¶ Drink waters out of thine own cistern, and running waters out of thine own well.
But the earthen vessel wherein it is sodden shall be broken: and if it be sodden in a brasen pot, it shall be both scoured, and rinsed in water.
And the waters shall fail from the sea, and the river shall be wasted and dried up.
Let thy fountains be dispersed abroad, and rivers of waters in the streets.
A fountain of gardens, a well of living waters, and streams from Lebanon.
For the Lord thy God bringeth thee into a good land, a land of brooks of water, of fountains and depths that spring out of valleys and hills;

Let us pass, I pray thee, through thy country: we will not pass through the fields, or through the vineyards, neither will we drink of the water of the wells: we will go by the king’s high way, we will not turn to the right hand nor to the left, until we have passed thy borders.
Doth a fountain send forth at the same place sweet water and bitter?
Let them be only thine own, and not strangers’ with thee.
Let another man praise thee, and not thine own mouth; a stranger, and not thine own lips.
Lest strangers be filled with thy wealth; and thy labours be in the house of a stranger;
Behold, therefore I will bring strangers upon thee, the terrible of the nations: and they shall draw their swords against the beauty of thy wisdom, and they shall defile thy brightness.
Moreover of the children of the strangers that do sojourn among you, of them shall ye buy, and of their families that are with you, which they begat in your land: and they shall be your possession.
Let me alone, that I may destroy them, and blot out their name from under heaven: and I will make of thee a nation mightier and greater than they.
Let thy fountain be blessed: and rejoice with the wife of thy youth.
Behold, I stand by the well of water; and it shall come to pass, that when the virgin cometh forth to draw water, and I say to her, Give me, I pray thee, a little water of thy pitcher to drink;

Farewell, my dear Thankful, thou wife of my youth, and mother of
my first born; the beginning of my strength--farewell. Yet a few
more lingering years of sorrow, pain and toil, and I shall be with
thee, and clasp thee to my bosom, and thou shalt sit down on my
throne, as a queen and priestess unto thy lord, arrayed in white
robes of dazzling splendor and decked with precious stones and
gold,…
Blessed shall be thy basket and thy store.
Let her be as the loving hind and pleasant roe; let her breasts satisfy thee at all times; and be thou ravished always with her love.
That ye may suck, and be satisfied with the breasts of her consolations; that ye may milk out, and be delighted with the abundance of her glory.
¶ We have a little sister, and she hath no breasts: what shall we do for our sister in the day when she shall be spoken for?
Exalt her, and she shall promote thee: she shall bring thee to honour, when thou dost embrace her.
And seest among the captives a beautiful woman, and hast a desire unto her, that thou wouldest have her to thy wife;
And why wilt thou, my son, be ravished with a strange woman, and embrace the bosom of a stranger?
My son, if thou be surety for thy friend, if thou hast stricken thy hand with a stranger,
Now therefore know and consider what thou wilt do; for evil is determined against our master, and against all his household: for he is such a son of Belial, that a man cannot speak to him.
To reveal his Son in me, that I might preach him among the heathen; immediately I conferred not with flesh and blood:
O that thou wert as my brother, that sucked the breasts of my mother! when I should find thee without, I would kiss thee; yea, I should not be despised.

And thou didst hear me because of mine afflictions and my sincerity; and it is because of thy Son that thou hast been thus merciful unto me, therefore I will cry unto thee in all mine afflictions, for in thee is my joy; for thou hast turned thy judgments away from me, because of thy Son.
For the ways of man are before the eyes of the Lord, and he pondereth all his goings.
For his eyes are upon the ways of man, and he seeth all his goings.
Every way of a man is right in his own eyes: but the Lord pondereth the hearts.
Righteousness shall go before him; and shall set us in the way of his steps.
For he looketh to the ends of the earth, and seeth under the whole heaven;
¶ His own iniquities shall take the wicked himself, and he shall be holden with the cords of his sins.

And he shall come into the world to redeem his people; and he shall take upon him the transgressions of those who believe on his name; and these are they that shall have eternal life, and salvation cometh to none else.

For behold, he has his friends in iniquity, and he keepeth his guards about him; and he teareth up the laws of those who have reigned in righteousness before him; and he trampleth under his feet the commandments of God;
Yet if thou warn the wicked, and he turn not from his wickedness, nor from his wicked way, he shall die in his iniquity; but thou hast delivered thy soul.
He shall redeem their soul from deceit and violence: and precious shall their blood be in his sight.
thee from iniquity, and that thou shouldst become to Him a son, and a servant, and a minister of His presence.
He shall die without instruction; and in the greatness of his folly he shall go astray.
When the righteous turneth from his righteousness, and committeth iniquity, he shall even die thereby.
Nevertheless, if thou warn the wicked of his way to turn from it; if he do not turn from his way, he shall die in his iniquity; but thou hast delivered thy soul.
He shall not fail nor be discouraged, till he have set judgment in the earth: and the isles shall wait for his law.
And shall make him of quick understanding in the fear of the Lord; and he shall not judge after the sight of his eyes, neither reprove after the hearing of his ears.
And let him repent of all his folly, and clothe himself with charity; and cease to do evil, and lay aside all his hard speeches;
